latest.title: Strategic Streaming, Treasury and Asset Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1050H
Bluebird Mining Ventures Ltd
05 June 2026
5 June 2026
Bluebird Mining Ventures Ltd
("BMV" or the "Company")
Strategic Streaming, Treasury and Asset Update
Bluebird Mining Ventures Ltd (LSE: BMV), the gold streaming, mining and treasury company, is pleased to provide the following update regarding the continued execution of its streaming and treasury strategy, including operational progress across its streaming activities, treasury accumulation, and deployment of mining infrastructure.
Streaming and Operating Business Update
The Company continues to build its operating streaming business across Bitcoin and precious-metals-related cash flow opportunities.
During May 2026, the Company marked an important milestone for BMV with the delivery of its first full month of revenue generation from operating assets. While still early in the Company's development, this represents a significant step in the transition from portfolio construction towards recurring cash flow generation.
Management views this as an important milestone in the evolution of the business and a demonstration of the Company's ability to convert assets into recurring economic output.
Additionally, the Company continued to strengthen its operating platform through targeted hires across Operations, Commercial and Finance. These additions have been made while maintaining BMV's disciplined and lean operating model, with a continued emphasis on equity-based incentives and long-term alignment with shareholders.
The Company also completed the restructuring of the legacy Asian assets. This work forms part of a broader effort to simplify the business, strengthen the balance sheet and improve capital efficiency.
Over the past twelve months, substantial progress has been made across both the asset and liability side of the balance sheet. As a result, management believes as BMV approaches the second half of 2026, it does so from a significantly stronger financial position than at any point in its recent history.
Looking ahead, the Company remains focused on expanding its portfolio of productive assets, growing recurring yield and executing on several strategic initiatives that are expected to further enhance shareholder value. Additional updates regarding the Company's yield-generation strategies will be provided in due course.
Gold Streaming Opportunities
Development work continues on BMV's flagship gold streaming opportunity. The Company believes the asset has the potential to become a cornerstone investment within the portfolio and remains focused on advancing the opportunity in a disciplined manner.
In parallel, management continues to evaluate a number of additional streaming and royalty opportunities. Consistent with the Company's investment philosophy, priority is being given to transactions
capable of delivering near and medium-term cash generation,
that offer strong downside protection, attractive risk-adjusted returns and high-quality counterparties.
Treasury Activities
The Company continues to maintain exposure to both gold and Bitcoin through its treasury strategy. Management remains constructive on the long-term role of scarce monetary assets and continues to view treasury management as a core component of shareholder value creation.

Sath Ganesarajah, Chief Executive Officer of BMV, commented:
"Having become revenue generating in April, we are thrilled to have delivered our first full month of revenue generation in May. This milestone strongly validates the success of the restructuring activities we have been undertaking to date in supporting our transition into a focused gold streaming, mining and treasury company, and the business case for this transition."
For further information, please contact:
Bluebird Mining Ventures Ltd
Sath Ganesarajah, Chief Executive Officer
c/o +44 (0)20 4582 3500
Gracechurch Group
Harry Chathli, Alexis Gore, Anysia Virdi
+44 (0)20 4582 3500
About Bluebird Mining Ventures Ltd
Bluebird Mining Ventures (LSE: BMV) is a gold streaming, mining and treasury company. The Company's mission is to build and manage a gold-backed treasury through streaming agreements, providing investors with exposure to physical gold without the operational risk of mining.
BMV focuses on streams from producing assets within the ore-concentrate-to-bullion value chain. Its investments secure multi-year flows of gold that can be recycled into new transactions. This model enables scalable exposure to gold without capital expenditure, or execution risks.
Drawing on its heritage in gold, BMV combines the stability of physical bullion with the benefits of a scalable, disciplined business model. With a focus on prudent capital allocation and treasury management, BMV aims to deliver sustainable, long-term value for shareholders.
For more information, please visit:
www.bmvbtc.com

latest.title: Update on South Korean Gold Interests
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3354G
Bluebird Mining Ventures Ltd
01 June 2026
1 June 2026
Bluebird Mining Ventures Ltd
("BMV" or the "Company")
Update on South Korean Gold Interests
Bluebird Mining Ventures Ltd (LSE: BMV), the gold streaming, mining and treasury company, is pleased to announce that it has restructured its exposure to the South Korean assets through a Share Purchase Agreement ("SPA") with 1575275 B.C. Ltd, a newly formed company incorporated in British Columbia, Canada, founded by Latika Prasad, an experienced mining executive based in Vancouver.
Under the terms of the SPA, 1575275 B.C. Ltd. will assume direct ownership of the South Korean mining interests and responsibility for all future funding, associated liabilities and obligations relating to the assets. BMV will transfer the assets to 1575275 B.C. Ltd. for a nominal consideration and will retain a 2.5% Net Smelter Return from both South Korean projects, Gubong and Kochang. 1575275 B.C. Ltd will have the right to buy-out the royalties for USD 2.5 million at each project.
The assets currently lack a JORC-compliant Mineral Resource Estimate or production approvals. Whilst the geology remains attractive, BMV considers the assets to be at an early-stage of development and carrying exploration risk.
The Board believes restructuring the South Korean gold interests, while retaining exposure through a royalty, better aligns the Company with its strategic focus and materially simplifies its structure, reducing future administrative and audit complexity.
Sath Ganesarajah, Chief Executive Officer of BMV, commented:
"The restructure of our South Korean mining interests is an important step in BMV's transition into a focused gold streaming, mining and treasury company. While Gubong and Kochang remain geologically attractive assets, the permitting pathway, development timeline and capital requirements are no longer aligned with our strategic priority of building a lower-risk, cash-generative platform.
"By transferring the assets to an experienced Vancouver-based mining team while retaining a 2.5% royalty over each project, BMV preserves meaningful upside exposure without the ongoing operational, administrative and funding burden. This transaction completes the transition of our legacy owned South Korean gold interests into potential royalty revenue streams and allows management to focus capital and resources on scalable streaming and royalty opportunities capable of delivering recurring cash flow and long-term shareholder value."
For further information, please contact:
Bluebird Mining Ventures Ltd
Sath Ganesarajah, Chief Executive Officer
c/o +44 (0)20 4582 3500
Gracechurch Group
Harry Chathli, Alexis Gore, Anysia Virdi
+44 (0)20 4582 3500
About Bluebird Mining Ventures Ltd
Bluebird Mining Ventures (LSE: BMV) is a gold streaming, mining and treasury company. The Company's mission is to build and manage a gold-backed treasury through streaming agreements, providing investors with exposure to physical gold without the operational risk of mining.
BMV focuses on streams from producing assets within the ore concentrate to bullion value chain. Its investments secure multi-year flows of gold that can be recycled into new transactions. This model enables scalable exposure to gold without capital expenditure, or execution risks.
Drawing on its heritage in gold, BMV combines the stability of physical bullion with the benefits of a scalable, disciplined business model. With a focus on prudent capital allocation and treasury management, BMV aims to deliver sustainable, long-term value for shareholders.
For more information, please visit: www.bmvbtc.com
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: BMV Ltd is a London‑listed investment vehicle that holds stakes in a variety of businesses across tech, mining and emerging digital assets. Its main activity is to acquire, hold and eventually sell shares in these companies to generate returns for its shareholders.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The firm sits at the intersection of traditional asset classes and the fast‑growing crypto‑mining sector. By owning stakes in gold‑mining and Bitcoin‑mining projects, it gives investors indirect exposure to two of the most talked‑about assets today while also leveraging its experience in managing portfolio companies.
company_30s.current_state: BMV has recently completed a £750k equity raise, admitted new shares, and updated its audit partner, signalling a focus on liquidity and governance. The company is still in a capital‑raising phase rather than a growth‑driven expansion phase. The share price is hovering near its 52‑week range, and the market has yet to react strongly to its latest filings.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy with routine corporate actions: it raised fresh capital, admitted new shares, and refreshed its audit partnership. These moves keep the balance sheet healthy but don’t provide a clear growth driver.

A second thread is the expansion into crypto‑related assets – a Texas Bitcoin project and a stake in South Korean gold interests. These are the only indications that BMV is looking to diversify into high‑volatility sectors that could drive future upside.

Regular updates (annual report, business update, management incentives) show the board is maintaining transparency, but the lack of a new product launch or major acquisition keeps the company’s narrative muted.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but curious – traders are watching for a catalyst, not reacting yet.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders whisper that the Bitcoin project could turn the company into a crypto‑asset play. They see the capital raise as a green light for future expansion.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most voices point to the lack of concrete growth plans and the company’s heavy reliance on capital markets. They worry that the firm may be a passive holding vehicle rather than an active growth engine.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been flat. The chatter is dominated by neutral posts about routine filings. No clear shift toward optimism or pessimism has emerged.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent interest spikes, giving the stock a surprise edge if a catalyst materialises.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of the Texas Bitcoin mining operation – including a signed contract or a funding commitment – could act as a trigger.
Any significant increase in trading volume at the 52‑week low or a bounce above the 50‑day moving average would signal that capitulation is done.
Positive guidance from the company’s quarterly earnings, especially if revenue growth or a new partnership is disclosed, would strengthen the case.
expect_next.watch_for: Monitor the 52‑week low for a sustained 3‑day volume spike and keep an eye on the next earnings release for any mention of the Bitcoin project or new investment opportunities.
expect_next.timing_regime: BMV is still in a pre‑catalyst phase. The APEX score of 8 suggests a tight price compression, but without a confirmed crash depth we cannot say whether the company is at the bottom of a 70‑95% drop. We need a clear event to confirm mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – insufficient evidence of a deep crash or imminent catalyst.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the Bitcoin project is launched and the company shows a clear path to revenue from crypto mining, the upside could be substantial. A 50% recovery from the current low would translate into a 100% gain if the stock is 70% down.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a tangible growth engine, the firm risks remaining a passive holding vehicle. The only realistic downside is the current market price, which could fall further if the company fails to secure a meaningful catalyst.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: up to 200% if the Bitcoin project materialises and the price rebounds to 50‑60% of its peak. Potential downside: the current market price, which could decline 10‑20% before any catalyst.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed volume spike at the 52‑week low and a bullish candle on the daily chart. Use a position size that limits exposure to 5% of the portfolio. Place a stop 10% below the entry to cap losses.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for the next earnings report or a formal announcement of the Bitcoin project. If the price stays below the 50‑day moving average and volume remains flat, hold off.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk is apparent at this time, so the setup remains viable.

ai_insights.bottom_line: BMV is a passive holding vehicle with a potential crypto catalyst that could unlock upside, but until a clear event confirms a deep crash has ended, the setup remains neutral.
